title: SessionIssues
description: Root-level structured verifier-findings artifact for one findings-bearing verification round. Written beside sN-verification.md as sN-issues.json (round 1) or sN-issues-round-<M>.json (later findings-bearing retries). This artifact has no runtime reader in the orchestration layer; it is durable, machine-readable persistence of the verifier's structured issue list. The presence of an sN-issues*.json file means that verification round found issues — VERIFIED rounds get no file. schemaVersion 1 (Set 055) keeps category/severity/resolution_status as loose strings. schemaVersion 2 (Set 057) promotes four optional finding fields (issueId, issueType, verificationMethod, suggestedTestOrCheck) and enum-enforces resolution_status + issueType when present; the fields stay advisory in semantics (no runtime gate reads them) — the enum is a spelling-drift guard. v1 files remain valid unchanged. See docs/session-issues-schema.md.

schemaVersion.description: Envelope schema version. 1 = the v1 contract locked in Set 055 (loose advisory strings). 2 = the v2 contract locked in Set 057 (promoted optional finding fields + enum-enforced resolution_status / issueType).

sessionNumber.description: 1-based session number within the set that this findings round belongs to.

verificationRound.description: 1-based verification round. Round 1 maps to sN-issues.json; round M>1 maps to sN-issues-round-<M>.json.

verificationVerdict.description: The verifier verdict for this round. Because a clean round writes no file, a persisted artifact is expected to carry a non-VERIFIED verdict; the field is preserved verbatim so the artifact is self-describing.

issues.description: The verifier's structured issue list for this round. At least one issue is present (the file would not exist otherwise). Under schemaVersion 2 each item additionally validates against IssueV2 (enum-enforced resolution_status / issueType, plus the optional promoted finding fields).

Issue.description: One verifier finding (base / v1 contract). The verifier-emitted fields are preserved verbatim; description is the only reliable required field. category and severity are loose optional strings (the parser may emit 'unknown'). The resolution_* fields are optional, append-only, orchestrator-side annotations and are advisory only — they are NOT authoritative workflow state and no runtime gate consults them.

resolution_status.description: Advisory annotation: what the orchestrator did with this finding. Loose string under schemaVersion 1; enum-enforced under schemaVersion 2 (see IssueV2).

resolution_notes.description: Advisory free-text annotation explaining the disposition of this finding.

resolved_in_round.description: Advisory annotation: the verification round in which this finding was resolved.

IssueV2.description: One verifier finding under the Set 057 v2 contract. Inherits the v1 base (description required, additionalProperties open) and adds: enum-enforced resolution_status / issueType WHEN PRESENT, plus four optional promoted finding fields (issueId, issueType, verificationMethod, suggestedTestOrCheck). All promoted fields stay OPTIONAL in the shared schema (additive / Full-tier-safe). The Lightweight dedicated-verification flow imposes a stricter requirement on a verifier-created open issue (issueId + issueType + verificationMethod) at the flow layer, NOT in this shared schema.

resolution_status.description: Advisory annotation, enum-enforced under schemaVersion 2 (semantics stay advisory; the enum is a spelling-drift guard). No runtime gate reads it.

issueId.description: Optional stable identifier the verifier assigns a finding so re-verification rounds and remediation sessions can reference it unambiguously.

issueType.description: Optional promoted classification of the finding, enum-enforced when present. deterministic-defect = a reproducible defect; contingent-risk = a conditional/latent risk; standards-departure = a convention/standards violation; missing-context = the verifier lacked context to judge.

verificationMethod.description: Optional one-line description of how the verifier reached this finding (the check it ran / would run). Required on a verifier-created open issue at the Lightweight dedicated-verification flow layer.

suggestedTestOrCheck.description: Optional concrete test or check that would confirm the finding is fixed. Stays optional even in the dedicated-verification flow (often redundant with verificationMethod).
